primer:
## How this set works
Fundamental constants and their values across **unit systems**. Three ideas first:
- **SI** (m, kg, s, J) is the default; **Gaussian-cgs** (cm, g, s, erg) differs mostly in electromagnetism (charge in esu, no $\varepsilon_0$).
- **Natural units** set $c=\hbar=1$ (often $k_B=1$): lengths/times/masses/energies share dimensions, so masses are quoted in **eV/$c^2$**.
- **Dimensionless** constants (like $\alpha$) are pure numbers — the **same in every system**.

## The constants
- **c** (speed of light): $2.998\times10^8$ m/s · $2.998\times10^{10}$ cm/s · $=1$ natural
- **h**: $6.626\times10^{-34}$ J·s · $6.626\times10^{-27}$ erg·s; **ħ** $=h/2\pi=1.055\times10^{-34}$ J·s ($=1$ natural)
- **G**: $6.674\times10^{-11}$ m³kg⁻¹s⁻² · $6.674\times10^{-8}$ cm³g⁻¹s⁻²
- **k_B**: $1.381\times10^{-23}$ J/K · $1.381\times10^{-16}$ erg/K ($=1$ natural); **R** $=N_A k_B=8.314$ J·mol⁻¹K⁻¹; **N_A** $=6.022\times10^{23}$ mol⁻¹; **σ** $=5.670\times10^{-8}$ W·m⁻²K⁻⁴
- **e**: $1.602\times10^{-19}$ C (SI) · $4.803\times10^{-10}$ esu (Gaussian); **ε₀** $=8.854\times10^{-12}$ F/m (absorbed in cgs); **μ₀** $\approx4\pi\times10^{-7}$ N·A⁻²; **α** $=e^2/4\pi\varepsilon_0\hbar c\approx1/137$
- **m_e** $=9.109\times10^{-31}$ kg $=0.511$ MeV/$c^2$; **m_p** $=1.673\times10^{-27}$ kg $=938.3$ MeV/$c^2$ ($\approx1836\,m_e$); **u** $=1.661\times10^{-27}$ kg $=931.5$ MeV/$c^2$
- **a₀** (Bohr radius) $=5.292\times10^{-11}$ m $=0.529$ Å; **1 eV** $=1.602\times10^{-19}$ J

Every card's answer is in this chart.
